data.title: Bursa Rallies Midday As All Major Indices Ride Broad-Based Buying
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia extended gains at midday, with all major indices advancing on broad-based buying.

The FBMKLCI rose 10.85 points or 0.62% to 1,752.11, while the FBM70 gained 198.16 points (+1.13%) to 17,796.75. The FBM Emas index added 97.69 points (+0.77%) to 12,854.24, and the FBM Shariah index increased 103.87 points (+0.84%) to 12,412.97. The F4GBM also climbed 8.21 points (+0.80%) to 1,036.20.

Trading activity remained focused on mid-cap and small-cap stocks. ZETRIX [0138] led in volume with 105 million shares, closing at RM0.84, slightly down 0.01, while TANCO [2429] inched up 0.01 to RM1.44 on 27.8 million shares. BPURI [5932] slipped 0.005 to RM0.295, 99SMART [5326] fell 0.17 to RM3.56, and CAPITALA [5099] gained 0.015 to RM0.61.

Top gainers included MPI [3867] up 0.92 to RM30.90 and Nestlé [4707] rising 0.70 to RM110.00, while BIPORT [5032] lost 0.20 to RM5.30 and DLADY [3026] dipped 0.20 to RM33.08.

Market sentiment remained cautiously optimistic, led by consumer staples and industrial stocks, as investors positioned ahead of corporate earnings and global cues, keeping all major indices in positive territory by midday.

data.title: Stock Today: Malakoff Rises Over 3% As Plant Concessions Extended To 2029
data.description:
Malakoff Corp Bhd climbed in afternoon trade on Thursday, rising 2.5 sen or 3.11% to 83 sen as at 2.45 pm, amid investor reaction to the extension of key power plant concessions.

The utility counter opened at 80.5 sen and moved between 80.5 sen and an intraday high of 84.5 sen, with some 2.08 million shares changing hands.

The stock’s latest price stood above its last done price of 80.5 sen previously, reflecting renewed buying interest. The buy queue was at 83 sen against a sell price of 83.5 sen.

The gain follows news that Malakoff secured extensions for three power plants under its subsidiaries until Dec 31, 2029. The assets comprise Segari Energy Ventures Sdn Bhd in Perak with a capacity of 1,303 megawatts, GB3 Sdn Bhd in Perak with 429 megawatts, and Prai Power Sdn Bhd in Penang with 350 megawatts.

The extensions strengthen earnings visibility for the independent power producer by ensuring continued operations of the combined 2,082 megawatts capacity over the next several years. Investors appeared to welcome the improved medium-term revenue certainty, pushing the stock higher during the session.

Malakoff, listed on the Main Market under the utilities sector, has seen its share price trend within the 70 sen to RM1.20 range over the past year. Thursday’s advance places the counter closer to the upper end of its recent trading band as the market digests the concession developments.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Lower Against US Dollar Following FOMC Minutes
data.description:
The ringgit eased against the US dollar at the opening on Thursday, as the greenback strengthened amid Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) minutes which signalled a potential interest rate hike, an analyst said.

At 8 am, the ringgit inched down to 3.8965/9160 versus the greenback compared to 3.8945/9055 at Monday’s close. 

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Dr Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the US Dollar Index (DXY) rose 0.57 per cent to 97.712 points as the latest FOMC minutes showed that several participants in the meeting would prefer a two-sided description of the committee’s decision.

“This situation suggested that raising the interest rate could be a possible outcome should the inflation remain above target level,” he told Bernama today.

However, Mohd Afzanizam said the ringgit is expected to linger around RM3.89 to RM3.91 today given the strong data points for the Malaysian economy.

At the opening, the ringgit however traded higher against a basket of major currencies.

It appreciated versus the Japanese yen to 2.5191/5318 from 2.5356/5430 at Monday’s close, strengthened against the euro to 4.5936/6166 from 4.6204/6335 previously, and rose vis-à-vis the British pound to 5.2583/2846 from 5.3137/3287.

The local note traded mostly higher against its ASEAN peers.

The ringgit improved versus the Singapore dollar to 3.0746/0905 from 3.0860/0949 last Monday, was higher vis-a-vis the Thai baht at 12.4640/5364 compared to 12.5205/5619 previously, and edged up against the Indonesian rupiah to 230.7/232.0 from 231.4/232.2.

However, it slipped against the Philippine peso to 6.73/6.77 from 6.71/6.74 at the last close.

Bernama

data.title: MPI Tops Gainers Board As 99Smart Slips In Early Trades
data.description:
Malaysian Pacific Industries Berhad (MPI) topped the gainers’ board today, climbing 80 sen to RM30.78 on strong trading volume of 3.07 million shares.

Allianz Malaysia Bhd (Allianz) followed with a 40 sen gain to RM22.66, while Petronas Dagangan Bhd (PetDag) rose 38 sen to RM21.00. Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd (HLFG) added 32 sen to RM22.32, and KGB Bhd (KGB) advanced 31 sen to RM5.11 with heavy trading of 31.34 million shares, rounding off the top five gainers.

On the downside, 99 Speedmart Holdings Bhd (99Smart) led the losers, retreating 23 sen to RM3.50 on extremely high volume of 38.65 million shares.

Southern Acids (M) Berhad (SAB) slipped 17 sen to RM3.28, while Tenaga Nasional Bhd (Tenaga) lost 16 sen to RM14.26. Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd (DLADY) eased 14 sen to RM33.14, and TGL Bhd (TGL) fell 7 sen to 83 sen, completing the top five decliners.

The session reflected mixed sentiment as investors favoured semiconductors and financials amid sector rotation, while retail and utilities saw profit-taking on elevated volumes.
​

data.title: Veteran Analysts Warns Of Bitcoin Crash To Below US$10,000
data.description:
Bitcoin the world’s first decentralised cryptocurrency, was launched in 2009 in the wake of the global financial crisis in 2008. The goal was to give financial autonomy to the people and liberate them from the clutches of Wall Street.

But the way Bitcoin has increasingly become integrated into the mainstream financial system, macroeconomic forces now tend to affect King Crypto’s price trajectory.That is exactly what happened when U.S. President Donald Trump’s tariff threat against China led to a massive liquidation in the crypto market on Oct. 10 last year.

Bitcoin had hit its all-time high (ATH) of $126,080 only a few days earlier on Oct. 6 and it is currently trading 45% lower.Earlier this month, Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan listed six major factors behind the crash in the crypto market.

First, long-term crypto investors have been selling to front-run the four-year cycle. The general thesis is that crypto has historically followed four-year cycles, with down years being 2014, 2018, and 2022 previously. So, traders think 2026 is also going to be a down year and are therefore exiting markets after making gains.

Second, though crypto remained the “most exciting, dynamic, and volatile segment” of the market, investors have now flocked to other exciting segments like artificial intelligence (AI) and precious metals.hree, the crypto market took the blow of Trump’s tariff threat alone, as Oct. 10 was followed by a weekend when Wall Street was closed for trading.

Four, Trump has nominated Kevin Warsh to be the next Federal Reserve chairman, who is seen to hold very hawkish views.

Five, there is a significant section of the Bitcoin community that fears the threat of quantum computing is near and not enough is being done to address the risk.

Six, Bitcoin has suffered from the broader market’s risk-off shift.

On Feb. 16, Bloomberg Intelligence strategist Mike McGlone warned that Bitcoin could even drop to $10,000.

While other analysts may talk of a “healthy correction” soon, a further decline is here, he said. But the buy-the-dip mantra since 2008 may be over, he added

The Street

data.title: Gold Slips To Below US$5000 As CNY Holiday Pauses Trade
data.description:
Gold slipped further below $5,000 an ounce in thin trading, with much of Asia closed for the Lunar New Year and after a US holiday on Monday.

Bullion fell as much as 1.4% on Tuesday, having lost 1% in the previous session. The metal had rallied briefly on Friday when modest US inflation data boosted the case for the Federal Reserve to trim interest rates. Lower borrowing costs are a tailwind for non-yielding precious metals.

A wave of speculative buying pushed a multiyear rally to breaking point in late January, with gold surging to a record above $5,595 an ounce. An abrupt, two-day rout at the turn of the month pulled the metal back to near $4,400, but it has since regained roughly half of its losses. Trading has been choppy since.

Many banks — including BNP Paribas SA, Deutsche Bank AG and Goldman Sachs Group Inc. — have forecast that prices will resume their upward trend, with the factors that underpinned gold’s steady ascent set to persist. These include heightened geopolitical tensions, questions over the Fed’s independence and a wider shift away from currencies and sovereign bonds.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates Feb 19, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5 pm on Feb 12, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Bursa Malaysia Opens Firmer After CNY Break
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened slightly higher as trading resumed after the Chinese New Year holidays, coinciding with the first day of Ramadan.

At 9.03am, the benchmark FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBMKLCI) rose 4.74 points, or 0.27%, to 1,746.00 after moving between 1,743.63 and 1,748.08 in early trade.

Broader indices were also firmer. The FBM70 gained 36.77 points, or 0.21%, to 17,635.36. The FBMEMAS added 32.87 points, or 0.26%, to 12,789.42, while the FBMSHA rose 32.37 points, or 0.26%, to 12,341.47. The F4GBM index advanced 2.89 points, or 0.28%, to 1,030.88.

Trading activity was led by lower-priced stocks. TWL slipped half a sen to 2 sen with 127.35 million shares traded, while Hubline was unchanged at 3.5 sen with 28.6 million shares done. Magma rose 1 sen to 33 sen after 22.77 million shares changed hands. OCR was flat at 5 sen with 11.0 million shares traded, while MAG held steady at 17.5 sen with 11.0 million shares transacted.

Among gainers, Hong Leong Bank rose 16 sen to RM24.12, Telekom Malaysia added 15 sen to RM7.98, and Petronas Dagangan gained 8 sen to RM20.70. Duopharma Biotech advanced 6 sen to RM1.54, while IOI Properties rose 6 sen to RM3.57.

On the losing side, IGB Bhd fell 15 sen to RM3.65. 99 Speed Mart declined 9 sen to RM3.64 with 9.06 million shares traded. Sunway Construction slipped 5 sen to RM6.15, while Petronas Chemicals and Tenaga Nasional eased 4 sen each to RM3.39 and RM14.38 respectively.

Market participants said sentiment was supported by mild bargain hunting following the festive break, although trading volumes may remain measured as investors adjust positions at the start of Ramadan and look to external cues for direction.

data.title: Gold Holds Ground After Lunar New Year Lull
data.description:
Gold steadied in early Asian trading after two sessions of losses, with activity subdued as several regional markets remained closed for the Lunar New Year holidays.

Spot bullion was little changed at US$4,879.79 an ounce at 7:30 a.m. in Singapore, following a drop of more than 3% over the previous two sessions. The pullback came as the US dollar strengthened, with a key gauge of the currency rising as much as 0.4% on Tuesday before trimming gains. The Bloomberg Dollar Spot Index ended the prior session up 0.2% for the week.

Trading conditions have been volatile since a sharp selloff in gold at the start of the month, leaving investors closely monitoring signals on US monetary policy for direction. Lower borrowing costs tend to support non-yielding assets such as bullion, and prices briefly rebounded last week after softer inflation data encouraged expectations of rate cuts.

Comments from officials at the Federal Reserve remained in focus. Governor Michael Barr said on Tuesday that rates should remain steady “for some time,” until there is clearer evidence that inflation is moving toward the central bank’s 2% target. Meanwhile, Chicago Fed President Austan Goolsbee said further rate cuts could be possible this year if inflation continues to ease toward that goal.

Other precious metals were mixed. Silver was little changed at US$73.52 an ounce, while platinum rose 0.5% and palladium gained 0.4%, reflecting cautious positioning across the metals complex as investors weigh the outlook for US interest rates and the dollar.

Bloomberg

data.title: SGX Opens Higher, STI Climbs 1% At The Bell
data.description:
The Singapore Exchange opened firmer on Thursday, with the benchmark Straits Times Index (STI) rising 49.92 points, or 1.01%, to 4,988.50 as at 9.00 am.

Market breadth was positive in early trade, with 101 advancers outpacing 48 decliners. Turnover stood at 45.10 million shares valued at S$124.12 million.

Among index heavyweights, DBS Group Holdings Ltd rose 0.37% to S$57.10 shortly after the open. Other actively watched counters included Singapore Telecommunications Ltd at S$4.96, Oversea-Chinese Banking Corporation at S$21.42, and United Overseas Bank at S$38.47. Keppel Ltd traded at S$12.80, while ST Engineering stood at S$10.19.

In the broader derivatives market, total volume reached 9,440 contracts in early trade. MSCI Singapore Index Futures for February were quoted at 459.05, while Nikkei 225 Index Futures for March stood at 57,560.00.

The upbeat start comes amid generally positive sentiment in regional markets, with investors monitoring currency futures including USD/SGD at 1.2621 and USD/CNH at 6.8819.

The STI remains just shy of the 5,000 mark as investors position ahead of further regional and global market catalysts.

data.title: Dollar Firms As Fed Minutes Signal No Rush To Cut Rates
data.description:
The US dollar held on to overnight gains in Asian trading on Thursday after minutes from the Federal Reserve’s latest meeting indicated policymakers were in no hurry to cut interest rates, with several even open to further hikes if inflation remains persistent.

The euro remained below US$1.18 at US$1.1788, weighed down both by the stronger dollar and a report that European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde plans to step down before her term ends next October. Sterling traded at US$1.3497.

US Treasury yields rose following the release of the minutes, which showed officials divided over the future path of rates. While some policymakers expect productivity gains to help ease inflationary pressures, “most participants” cautioned that progress may be slow and uneven, with several signalling that rate increases remain possible if inflation stays above target.

“This suggests there isn’t a great deal of urgency to cut rates again, at least not until after current chair (Jerome) Powell’s term ends in May,” said Peter Dragicevich, Asia-Pacific currency strategist at Corpay.

The Japanese yen weakened around 1% overnight and was steady at 154.78 per dollar, retreating from last week’s 152 level following Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s electoral victory. The currency also came under pressure after the Trump administration announced US$36 billion worth of projects as part of Japan’s pledged US$550 billion investment in the United States.

“Direct Japanese investment into the U.S. will be a key watch factor this year, and one which adds to the very mixed picture on USD/JPY,” said Chris Turner, global head of research at ING. “The question for FX markets this year is whether this investment proves a supportive dollar flow or something like Japan’s FX reserves are used to guarantee new USD loans and avoid pressure on the yen. The latter seems to be the preferred outcome for Tokyo.”

Elsewhere, the Australian dollar traded at US$0.7045 ahead of employment data that could shape expectations for further rate increases. The New Zealand dollar fell nearly 1.4% overnight — its steepest decline since April last year — after the central bank adopted a more cautious stance on future tightening, leaving the kiwi just below US$0.60.

Trading volumes in Asia were lighter due to holidays in Hong Kong, mainland China and Taiwan. The offshore yuan held steady at 6.89 per dollar.

Markets are now looking ahead to global purchasing managers’ index readings and US gross domestic product data due on Friday for further direction.

Reuters

data.title: Stock Today: Press Metal Inches Higher As FY25 Core Profit Breakthrough Looms
data.description:
Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd (PMETAL) rose slightly to RM7.69, up 0.13% or RM0.01, on Thursday with a traded volume of 2.4 million shares, reflecting cautious optimism ahead of the group’s 4Q25 results.

The stock opened at RM7.67, touched an intraday high of RM7.74 and a low of RM7.60, while the last done price aligned with the lower end of the buy-sell spread at RM7.69.

RHB Research maintains its BUY call on Press Metal with a target price of RM8.50, highlighting strong fundamentals underpinned by elevated global aluminium prices and favourable input costs.

Analysts project FY25 core profit after tax and minority interests to surpass RM2 billion for the first time, driven by a fourth-quarter PATAMI expected between RM650 million and RM730 million.

The rally is underpinned by an 8% quarter-on-quarter rise in LME aluminium prices to an average of US$2,830 per tonne and a 10% drop in alumina costs to US$319 per tonne, improving the “alumina-to-aluminium” cost ratio to roughly 10%, well below the three-year average of 17%.

Despite recent volatility triggered by U.S. Federal Reserve chair speculation, RHB analysts describe the group’s underlying fundamentals as “rock solid,” further supported by the scheduled wind-down of the 560,000-tonne Mozal plant in Mozambique.

While a strengthening Ringgit poses a minor headwind for Press Metal’s US$-denominated exports, the company’s low-cost hydropower-powered smelting and its role as a proxy for global green metal supply-demand dynamics continue to underpin investor confidence.

With a three-year CAGR of 27%, Press Metal remains a preferred pick for exposure to the global energy transition.

data.title: Dollar Holds Gains In Thin Trading As Market Awaits Fed Minutes, US GDP
data.description:
The dollar held gains on Tuesday as markets awaited signals, expected later this week, about the potential timing of rate cuts by the Federal Reserve.

The yen trimmed losses from a day earlier when worse-than-expected Japanese economic data stirred expectations that the government would ramp up stimulus. The Aussie dollar edged lower after the release of Reserve Bank of Australia’s minutes from its February meeting.

Trading was thin with many markets in Asia closed for the Lunar New Year holiday and following the President’s Day holiday in the U.S. Key economic events lie later in the week, with minutes from the Fed’s last meeting and advance figures on U.S. gross domestic product.

“We’re quite positive on the U.S. economy,” said Kristina Clifton, senior currency strategist at Commonwealth Bank of Australia in Sydney. “The market is currently pricing a high chance of a June interest rate cut, which is also our view. However, we differ from the market in that we expect a follow-up cut in July.”  

“We judge that the most important driver of the dollar through 2026 will be the narrative of U.S. exceptionalism,” she added.The dollar index, which measures the greenback against a basket of currencies, was little changed at 97.12, after a 0.2 per cent gain in the previous session. The euro fell 0.06 per cent to $1.1843.

The yen strengthened 0.15 per cent to 153.28 per dollar. Sterling weakened 0.07 per cent to $1.3616.

Data on Friday showed U.S. consumer prices increased less than expected in January, giving the Fed additional leeway for policy easing this year.

Money market traders are pricing in 62 basis points of easing for the rest of this year, implying two quarter-point cuts and about a 50 per cent chance of a third. The next cut is likely in June, with markets assigning an 80 per cent chance of a 25-basis-point reduction.

The Federal Open Market Committee issues minutes from its January meeting on Wednesday. Other key data points this week include inflation readings for Britain, Canada and Japan, as well as preliminary readings of global business activity on Friday.

A recent rally in the yen stalled on Monday when official figures showed Japan’s economy barely grew last quarter, eking out an annualised 0.2 per cent expansion.

The Australian dollar weakened 0.07 per cent versus the greenback to $0.7064. New Zealand’s kiwi weakened 0.08 per cent to $0.6026 ahead of the Reserve Bank of New Zealand’s policy meeting on Wednesday, when it is widely expected to hold rates steady.

Australia’s central bank concluded inflation would stay stubbornly high if it had not hiked interest rates as it did this month and it was not yet sure if further tightening would be necessary.

Minutes of the RBA’s board meeting last month showed members were worried that the risks to its inflation and employment mandates had “shifted materially.” 

In cryptocurrencies, bitcoin gained 0.05 per cent to $68,881.72, while ether was little changed at $1,999.11.

Reuters

data.title: Late Tech Rally Pushes US Stocks To Modest Gains
data.description:
US stocks finished slightly higher on Tuesday after a volatile session, with a late rebound in technology shares and strength in financial counters helping major indices recover from earlier losses.

The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose 32.26 points, or 0.07%, to 49,533.19. The S&P 500 gained 7.05 points, or 0.10%, to 6,843.22, while the Nasdaq Composite added 31.71 points, or 0.14%, to 22,578.38.

Technology shares staged a turnaround after earlier weakness, with gains in Nvidia and Apple offsetting declines in Microsoft and Oracle. The S&P 500 information technology sector, which had fallen as much as 1.5% during the session, ended 0.5% higher.

Financial stocks also provided support, with banks including Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase advancing and helping the Dow recover from an earlier decline of about 0.7%.

Despite the rebound in some large-cap technology names, pressure persisted in parts of the software segment. The S&P 500 software index dropped 1.6%, with Intuit and Cadence Design Systems among the steepest decliners, each falling more than 5%. Consumer staples were the weakest sector overall, dragged lower by a 7% slide in General Mills after the company cut its annual core sales and profit forecasts.

Market sentiment has been unsettled in recent weeks after worries that advances in artificial intelligence could disrupt business models across several industries. Concerns intensified after Alibaba Group unveiled its new AI model, Qwen 3.5, designed to independently carry out complex tasks.

“There’s a lot of different trends going on in terms of where investors want to put money right now and you see that in this market where you just see spikes up and spikes down, on maybe not a daily basis, but on a regular basis,” said Tim Ghriskey, senior portfolio strategist at Ingalls & Snyder in New York.

“The market is looking very short-term here and there will be a return to AI plays being very much in favor.”

Investors are now turning their attention to upcoming economic data, particularly the personal consumption expenditure report, the Federal Reserve’s preferred inflation gauge. Cooler-than-expected consumer inflation figures released last week have already lifted expectations of interest-rate cuts later this year, with traders pricing in roughly a 63% chance of at least a 25-basis-point reduction by June.

Several corporate developments also moved individual stocks. Shares of Norwegian Cruise Line Holdings surged 12.1% after activist investor Elliott disclosed a stake exceeding 10%. Fiserv jumped 6.9% following reports that Jana Partners had taken a position, while Masimo soared 34.2% after Danaher Corporation agreed to acquire the pulse-oximeter maker for US$9.9 billion including debt.

Trading breadth was mixed. Advancers slightly outnumbered decliners on the New York Stock Exchange, while decliners held a narrow lead on the Nasdaq. Total trading volume on US exchanges reached 17.76 billion shares, below the 20-day average of about 20.7 billion.

data.title: Gold Pauses After Rally Amid Strong Dollar And Lunar New Year Lull
data.description:
Gold prices slipped on Thursday in a thinly traded market following a more than 2% gain in the previous session, pressured by a firm US dollar ahead of key inflation data that could influence the Federal Reserve’s interest rate decisions.

Spot gold fell 0.2% to US$4,968.10 per ounce, while U.S. April gold futures declined 0.4% to US$4,987.60.

“Gold is in a consolidation mood, reflective of thin liquidity conditions during the Asia holiday period rather than a shift in fundamentals,” said Christopher Wong, strategist at OCBC.

Markets across Mainland China, Hong Kong, Singapore, Taiwan and South Korea remained closed for Lunar New Year holidays, contributing to the subdued trading. The dollar stayed near a one-week high, making bullion priced in greenbacks more expensive for holders of other currencies.

January Federal Reserve minutes indicated near-unanimous agreement to hold interest rates steady, though policymakers remain divided on future moves, with some open to hikes if inflation persists and others leaning towards cuts should inflation ease.

Market participants currently anticipate the first rate reduction this year in June, according to CME’s FedWatch Tool.

Investors now await weekly US jobless claims and Friday’s Personal Consumption Expenditures report, the Fed’s preferred inflation gauge.

Wong added that near-term consolidation is expected before gold and silver trend higher, with gold likely trading between US$4,800 and US$5,100 per ounce and silver in the US$70 to US$90 range.

Spot silver held at US$77.18 per ounce after a more than 5% rise on Wednesday, while platinum eased 0.1% to US$2,069.58 and palladium remained steady at US$1,715.93.

Reuters

data.title: Bursa Extends Upward Momentum
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia continued its upward trend, closing higher on Thursday due to positive market sentiment driven by stronger-than-expected macroeconomic data.

The bourse also benefitted from gains on Wall Street overnight and firmer regional markets.

The market was closed on Feb 17 and Feb 18 for the Chinese New Year holidays.

At 5 pm, the FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) rose by 10.85 points, or 0.62 per cent, to 1,752.11 compared with Monday’s close of 1,741.26.

The benchmark index had opened 6.82 points higher at 1,748.08, and moved between 1,755.08 and 1,743.63 throughout the trading session.

The broader market was also positive with gainers thumping losers 748 to 288, while 483 counters were unchanged, 1,218 counters untraded, and 11 suspended.

Turnover was higher at 2.04 billion units, worth RM2.52 billion, compared to 1.81 billion units, worth RM1.81 billion, on Monday.

IPPFA Sdn Bhd director of investment strategy and country economist Mohd Sedek Jantan said most regional equity markets closed higher, reinforcing the constructive risk tone that carried into the local benchmark index.

“Major indices finished in positive territory, reflecting broad-based buying interest. Among benchmark constituents, banking stocks led the gainers on renewed risk appetite, while utilities counters lagged amid sector rotation.

“On a sectoral basis, the energy index outperformed, advancing by more than two per cent, driven by a surge of over seven per cent in selected green energy stocks,” he told Bernama.

Mohd Sedek also said that the United States markets ended higher in the previous session as manufacturing resilience re-entered the narrative, with stronger-than-expected durable goods orders and industrial production.

“On the economic front, today’s release of Malaysia’s January consumer price index (CPI) data showed headline inflation steady at 1.6 per cent year-on-year, with core inflation unchanged at 2.3 per cent, reflecting a balance of opposing price pressures rather than an absence of movement.

“This upward pressure was offset by declining transport and energy prices, which moved into negative territory during the month, exerting a countervailing influence on the headline index,” he added.

Among the heavyweights, Maybank gained 12 sen to RM12.06, Public Bank rose 10 sen to RM5.06, CIMB and IHH Healthcare added nine sen each to RM8.47 and RM8.91, respectively, while Tenaga Nasional fell 16 sen to RM14.26.

On the most active list, Zetrix AI shed half a sen to 85.5 sen, Tanco and Capital A advanced one sen each to RM1.44 and 60.5 sen, respectively, Sime Darby climbed six sen to RM2.40, while 99 Speed Mart slipped 23 sen to RM3.50.

As for the top gainers, Malaysian Pacific Industries climbed 80 sen to RM30.78, Allianz Malaysia grew 40 sen to RM22.66, Petronas Dagangan bagged 38 sen to RM21, Hong Leong Financial expanded 32 sen to RM22.32, and Kelington garnered 31 sen to RM5.11.

Besides 99 Speed Mart and Tenaga Nasional, other top decliners included Southern Acids, which slipped 17 sen to RM3.28, Dutch Lady Milk lost 14 sen to RM33.14, and Teo Guan Lee dipped seven sen to 83 sen.

On the index board, the FBM Top 100 Index expanded 99.25 points to 12,678.47, the FBM Emas Index notched up 103.92 points to 12,860.47, the FBM Mid 70 Index increased 233.21 points to 17,831.80, the FBM Emas Shariah Index improved 94.69 points to 12,403.79, and the FBM ACE Index added 55.72 points to 4,828.51.

Sector-wise, the Financial Services Index was up by 206.68 points to 21,565.10, the Plantation Index grew 41.96 points to 8,402.31, the Energy Index rose 19.29 points to 774.11, and the Industrial Products and Services Index inched up 1.24 points to 178.50.

 The Main Market volume climbed up to 1.35 billion units worth RM2.37 billion from 1.15 billion units worth RM1.68 billion on Monday.

Warrant turnover expanded to 467.07 million units worth RM53.90 million from 401.15 million units worth RM49.71 million previously.

The ACE Market volume advanced to 227.62 million units valued at RM97.82 million from 259.17 million units valued at RM80.65 million on Monday.

Consumer products and services counters accounted for 224.63 million shares traded on the Main Market, industrial products and services (147.27 million), construction (117.67 million), technology (275.17 million), financial services (108.28 million), property (159.68 million), plantation (16.13 million), real estate investment trusts (26.36 million), closed-end fund (nil), energy (94.67 million), healthcare (56.44 million), telecommunications and media (47.57 million), transportation and logistics (43.29 million), utilities (27.71 million), and business trusts (735,400).

data.title: Copper Slips As Stockpiles Surge During Lunar New Year Lull
data.description:
Industrial metals started the week on the back foot, with copper and aluminium slipping in thin Lunar New Year trading. With many Asian traders still away from their desks, the market lacked momentum. According to Bloomberg, rising stockpiles and uncertainty over US import tariffs are weighing on sentiment.

Copper dropped as much as 0.8% to below $12,800 a tonne. Inventories tracked by the London Metal Exchange have climbed to their highest level in more than two decades, adding pressure to prices. Aluminium fell by up to 0.6% as investors waited for clearer signals on Washington’s tariff plans. US markets are due to reopen on Tuesday after the Presidents’ Day holiday.

High prices have already cooled demand in China, the world’s biggest metals consumer. Stockpiles are rebuilding after heavy shipments to the US ahead of expected tariffs. Combined copper inventories across exchanges in Shanghai, London and New York are now well above one million tonnes, the highest level in decades based on exchange data.

The LMEX Index, which tracks six key base metals in London, hit a record last month on strong Chinese buying and a weaker dollar. It has since pulled back as traders look for fresh direction, including updates on tariffs and signals from the US Federal Reserve.

There was some brighter news from the mining sector. BHP Group said first-half earnings jumped by more than 20%, helped by years of investment in copper production. The metal now makes up more than half of the company’s profits, offsetting weaker performance in its iron ore and coal businesses.

By late morning in Shanghai, copper futures were down 0.6% at $12,775.50 a tonne. Aluminium fell 0.5% to $3,037.50, zinc lost 0.7% to $3,268, and nickel edged 0.1% lower.

data.title: Asian Stocks To Climb As Tech Lifts US Equities
data.description:
Asian equities were set to open higher Thursday after a rebound in tech and robust economic data lifted US stocks. Higher Treasury yields supported the dollar, while oil jumped.

Equity index futures for Japan and Australia rose, while markets in China remained closed for the Lunar New Year holiday. The S&P 500 gained 0.6% Wednesday and the tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 advanced 0.8%. A gauge of chipmakers climbed 1% and an ETF tracking software firms jumped 1.3%.

The action in New York trading offered a sign that concerns over the disruption of AI were easing, just as several stock pickers surveyed the wreckage for buying opportunities.

The software stock selloff is likely “overdone” as that was a largely knee-jerk reaction, with investors trying to figure out the winners and losers from AI, according to Paul Stanley at Granite Bay Wealth Management.

“While AI is very promising, investors should not assume that all companies will win on the AI front,” he said.

Wednesday’s data showing the biggest increase in US industrial production in January sweetened the mood, while orders for business equipment rose in December by more than projected and housing starts hit a five-month high.

Stock market bulls were even unfazed by minutes of the Federal Open Market Committee’s January meeting that revealed “several participants” said they would have preferred a post-meeting statement that raised the possibility of raising the federal funds rate “if inflation remains at above-target levels.”

Fed funds futures pricing on Wednesday indicated traders slightly pared bets on rate cuts this year but still expect a further two 25 basis-point reductions in 2026.

“From our perspective, the minutes support our view that rate cuts are off the table for the foreseeable future,” said Charlie Ripley at Allianz Investment Management.

The US 10-year yield rose just over two basis points to around 4.08%, while the policy-sensitive two-year yield climbed three basis points to 3.46%. A $16 billion sale of 20-year bonds drew lackluster demand. The dollar gained 0.5% and gold advanced toward $5,000 an ounce.

In Asia, data set for release Thursday includes core machine orders for Japan, labor market data for Australia and interest rate decisions in the Philippines and Indonesia. Markets in China, Hong Kong, Taiwan and Vietnam are closed.

The yen had its worst day this month amid a broader selloff in major currencies as US economic data supported higher Treasury yields. Japan is set to sell 20-year bonds Thursday.

Oil gained the most since October as traders weighed whether talks between the US and Iran will be enough to avert conflict, following a report that American military intervention could come sooner than expected.

Bloomberg

data.title: Stock Today: CIMB Inches Up Amid Indonesia Stake Talks
data.description:
Shares of CIMB Group Holdings Bhd were trading higher on Thursday afternoon, gaining 0.84% as investors considered the potential divestment of its Indonesian unit.

As of 3.59 pm, the counter was last done at RM8.45, up seven sen from the previous close of RM8.38. The stock had touched a session high of RM8.48 and a low of RM8.41, with total traded volume at 130.48 million shares (’00 basis).

The market’s focus remains on CIMB’s 92.4% holding in PT Bank CIMB Niaga TBK, as Indonesian regulators plan to raise the minimum free float requirement on the Indonesia Stock Exchange from 7.5% to 15%, ahead of an MSCI reassessment in May.

Analysts suggest CIMB may reduce its stake by at least 7.4%, potentially generating one-off proceeds of around RM810 million based on Niaga’s current market capitalisation of approximately RM10.9 billion.

Despite Niaga historically contributing about 25% to CIMB’s profit before tax, the impact of a potential stake reduction is expected to be minimal, with projected declines of 1.2% and 2.0% for FY26 and FY27 respectively. Analysts note that any divestment proceeds could be used to expand the loan book or enhance shareholder returns, complementing the bank’s RM2 billion capital return plan through 2027.

Currency risk remains a factor, with every 1% depreciation of the Indonesian rupiah against its year-to-date average estimated to reduce CIMB’s profit before tax by about 0.3%.

data.title: Stock Picks: Petronas Chemicals And Duopharma
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) has identified Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd and Duopharma Biotech Bhd as near-term trading opportunities, citing improving technical indicators and strengthening momentum.

RHB Research said Petronas Chemicals is poised for further upside after testing immediate resistance with strong trading volume. The stock printed a long bullish candlestick on Monday, indicating that an interim base has formed near its 21-day simple moving average (SMA).

A decisive move above the RM3.45 resistance level could pave the way for gains towards RM3.70, followed by RM3.95. However, a break below the RM3.20 support level would likely weaken sentiment and signal a resumption of its corrective phase.

Meanwhile, Duopharma Biotech is seen extending its upward momentum after breaching the RM1.45 resistance level on a white candlestick, forming a fresh “higher high” close. RHB Research said this reaffirms the stock’s bullish rebound structure and suggests positive momentum remains intact.

The counter is expected to test the RM1.55 resistance, with further upside towards RM1.65 if sentiment continues to improve. On the downside, a fall below the RM1.35 support would dampen bullish momentum and potentially trigger a correction.

RHB Research’s technical outlook highlights selective trading opportunities amid improving broader market sentiment.
